options.label: A
options.text: Gas particles sitting still at the bottom of a container

options.label: B
options.text: Gas particles moving randomly and colliding with the container walls

options.label: C
options.text: The weight of the gas pushing down from above

options.label: D
options.text: Heat energy stored inside the gas particles

explanation: Gas pressure is created by billions of tiny particles moving rapidly in all directions and constantly bumping into the walls of their container. These collisions push on the walls and create the pressure we measure. This is why inflating a balloon makes it firm—more particles inside means more collisions and higher pressure.
